title: George United wins vital points against France United
description: SOCCER NEWS - On Saturday 30 August, George...
bodyText:
SOCCER NEWS - On Saturday 30 August, George United FC clinched a hard-fought 2-1 win against France United in a Geolfa Super League match at Lawaaikamp Stadium.


> The youthful Rooirivier side earned crucial points in their quest for a higher spot on the league table, with both teams vying for top positions.


George United dominated early, building play from the centre with precise passing. Tabiso Mlotywa shone brightly, creating multiple scoring chances and netting the opening goal to give his team a 1-0 lead. Ngcungela Bless added a second, ensuring a 2-0 advantage at half-time despite France United's dangerous free-kick attempts, which were thwarted by United's goalkeeper.

The second half saw France United rally, pressing for a comeback. Their efforts earned a penalty after a foul by United's backline, which Tsepo Martins converted to narrow the score to 2-1. France United pushed hard for an equaliser, but George United's defence, led by captain Jordan Burgess, held firm. United's midfield, with Luniko Xabe and Mlotywa dictating play, maintained control.

Despite France United's late surge, no further goals were scored. George United's resilience secured the 2-1 victory, earning them bragging rights and vital points in the tightly contested Geolfa Super League. The match showcased both teams' determination, with United's blend of youth and leadership proving decisive.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
